Ad Text:
Country Continuous Improvement Leader - IKEA Australia & New Zealand

Are you a passionate driver of change, eager to shape the future of retail by embedding a culture of continuous improvement. As the Country Continuous Improvement Leader, you'll be instrumental in advancing Lean methodologies across our operations, enhancing productivity, and delivering exceptional customer value in an evolving omnichannel landscape.

 

About Digital & Expansion

IKEA Retail, as the core business of Ingka, is dedicated to creating a better everyday life for many by ensuring long-term profitable growth, making IKEA more affordable, accessible, and sustainable. This commitment involves leading retail operations, serving happy customers, and developing IKEA as a purpose-led brand, while continuously expanding our presence to meet customers wherever and however they choose. We proactively set the digital agenda, driving the rapid development and operation of digital products and services using new technologies and agile methods, thereby fostering the continuous growth of our technical, people, and business skills to enhance our digital capabilities.

 

As the Country Continuous Improvement Leader for IKEA Australia & New Zealand, you will be responsible for driving Lean methodology and ways of working across the organisation. Your role will be to enable a robust continuous improvement capability, aiming to enhance productivity, quality, and efficiency, reduce waste, and ultimately increase both co-worker engagement and customer value by implementing Lean principles across all units and processes. Your responsibilities will include but are not limited to:

Identifying and prioritising key improvement areas with country business owners and management, aligning with the overall country roadmap and organisational goals. Support business owners and Business Navigation in calculating business potential and tracking progress, while enhancing lean ways of working cross-functionally.

Driving and empowering the implementation of Lean ways of working across units and Service Office to boost productivity, quality, efficiency, reduce waste, and increase co-worker engagement and customer value.

Supporting business owners in developing, implementing, and maintaining standardised processes across units to drive efficiency, quality, and continuous improvement.

Inspiring co-workers to adopt a strong everyday waste elimination mindset, and train leaders and end-users in Lean principles, methods, and tools.

Acting as the primary ambassador and go-to person for continuous improvement and Lean thinking across the country, coaching and challenging leaders and managers through change processes and in their daily operations.

Contributing to the global development of the Lean ways of working initiative, framework, and concept by participating in the Lean network and sharing learnings and challenges with colleagues.




About You

You bring deep knowledge of continuous improvement methodologies, particularly Lean, having actively applied its mindset, principles, methods, and tools within operations, backed by a solid retail business understanding and extensive experience. You also possess strong knowledge of how to create and implement change initiatives in complex environments & effectively manage stakeholders. You are an early adopter of technology, harnessing the power of AI in your practice. Furthermore, you excel at leading in uncertain environments, embracing a proactive, experimental, and solution-focused mindset, driving with high perseverance to deliver results and overcome obstacles. You are performance-driven, excel at building trustful relationships to lead through change, and apply end-to-end thinking and steer away from siloed approaches.

 

To be considered for this role you will have:

Proven experience in applying continuous improvement methodologies in operations.

Strong problem-solving skills, coupled with expertise in data analysis and fact-based decision-making.

Experience leveraging AI and automation to speed up organisational processes will be highly regarded.

Demonstrated training, facilitation, mentoring, and coaching skills.

Experience within a global organisation, leading in a complex business environment that includes both physical and digital operations and development.

Additional information:

Permanent, full-time, 76hrs/fortnight.

The location for the role is Service Office, Tempe NSW, with a minimum of 3 days a week on-site.

If you are successful for this role, you will be required to obtain a national police check.

Immediate start, 4-month contract.

Job Description

At SEEK, we’re reimagining what’s possible in work and careers. As a Senior Finance Business Partner, you’ll play a key role in helping our leaders make smart, informed decisions that drive SEEK’s success across Technology function.
Reporting to the Senior Finance Manager, you’ll manage the full planning and forecasting cycle, deliver strategic insights, and influence decisions that shape SEEK’s product and technology investments. Lead budgeting, forecasting and business case modelling that supports decision-making;

At SEEK, we are passionate about fostering a culture of inclusion and wellbeing that embraces and values the diversity of our people. We are a purpose driven business that works with heart.

SYPAQ is an industry leader in C4ISREW and autonomous systems integration, product development, and consulting services, with established engineering and IT teams across projects in Canberra, Melbourne, Adelaide, Sydney, and Brisbane. Our rich history of partnering with the Australian Defence, National Security community as well as major Federal Government departments continues to deliver a true sovereign industrial capability through advanced engineering and IT services and solutions.

SYPAQ is seeking a EL2 Lead SAP Analyst for an exciting opportunity within a Federal Government Department. This long term opportunity offers multiple extension options, up to 36 months and is available to be worked from either QLD, ACT & VIC.  Hybrid / Work From Home is available with a minimum expectation of 3 days per week in the office.

As the Lead Analyst your primary responsibility will be to translate business requirements into technical requirements that align with relevant architectures. You will operate with a significant degree of independence and work under the broad guidance of a National Manager to deliver quality outcomes. Operating with a high level of autonomy, with decision-making highly dependent on your own judgement, you will identify and drive innovation, manage and lead change, and have an active role in contributing to and implementing strategic direction.

You may specialise in the following areas:
SAP FI Technical Lead
SAP HR Technical Lead
Senior Basis Administrator
 
The SAP FI Technical Lead is responsible for managing the overall operation of the Financial and Procurement components of the SAP system, through the coordination of development and improvement activities. The SAP FI Technical Lead, under broad direction and in consultation with the Assistant Director, FI and Reporting Support, manages the assignment of development activities to staff and contractors, provides quality assurance of system changes, and provides technical advice and guidance for ongoing work and future planning for the SAP FI components.
 
SAP HR Technical Lead is responsible for implementing system changes and developments, under the broad coordination of the Assistant Director, Payroll Administration and HR Support. The SAP HR Technical Lead will work under broad direction to develop, test and document changes performed in their broad areas of expertise. The Technical Lead will also provide high level support for Human Resources and Payroll issues that cannot be resolved at the first or second level.
 
The Senior Basis Administrator is the subject matter expert responsible for ensuring the correct operation of the SAP application and its infrastructure. The Basis Administrator is responsible for, under broad direction, the planning and management of the effective technical implementation of SAP, including user and role management, including databases and servers.

 

Skills Required: 

Including but not limited to: 

• SAP FI 

• SAP HR 

• SAP Basis Administrator 

 

Preferred: 

• Extensive experience in the technical management of SAP Basis.  

• Extensive experience and configuration knowledge of SAP FI/HR in a large-scale organisation

Ad Text:
This is an exciting time to help lead our analysis of domestic economic developments

You will help lead a team of analysts responsible for the analysis of domestic economic activity, which feeds into the material presented to the Reserve Bank Board and to the public

Work in a collaborative and dynamic environment that allows flexible working arrangements and opportunities to grow


About the department

The Economic Analysis Department monitors and forecasts trends in domestic and international economies to brief the Monetary Policy Board, Governance Board and the public on the state of the economy. It plays an important role in making recommendations for monetary policy. Its two main responsibilities are the monthly Monetary Policy Board and Governance Board meeting and the Bank's key policy-related publication, the quarterly Statement on Monetary Policy (SMP).

It is responsible for a regular liaison program with businesses, governments and associations across different states and industries to collect information to complement data from official sources. The department manages the Bank's office in Beijing, which is responsible for monitoring economic and financial developments and maintaining relationships with government and private sector entities in China.

About the role

We are looking for a senior economist (manager) in the Domestic Activity and Trade section (DAT). DAT sits within the Bank's Economic Analysis department and is responsible for assessing developments in and producing forecasts for Australian economic activity and the housing market. The role forms part of a leadership team of three that leads and develops the team and provides analytical and strategic guidance on policy-relevant issues.

A key function of the role is to distil a wide set of information into a concise narrative that sets the domestic macroeconomic context for the Board's monetary policy decisions and public communication.

About you

We are looking for an adaptable team player to join the section and department leadership team. Given the nature of the team's work, the ideal candidate will possess the following:


Strong people leadership and influencing skills, and the ability to nurture an inclusive culture and team-based approach to work.

Strong macroeconomic frameworks and experience in economic analysis and research or in a closely related field.

Strong organisational skills and the ability to judge what issues are most important and to reprioritise quickly as circumstances change, and a passion to own the delivery of the team's regular outputs and analytical agenda.

Strong stakeholder engagement skills together with an ability to work collaboratively with other sections in the Bank and externally.

Strong communication skills, including the ability to identify the key issues that need to be conveyed to external audience.

Relevant tertiary qualifications in economics, finance or closely related field, or equivalent experience.


Next steps

To apply for this role, candidates should submit their resume and provide written responses to the following three short answer questions (maximum response is 200 words per question) in-lieu of a cover letter:

1.What is an example of an inclusion, diversity and belonging initiative that you have contributed to?

2. Describe your philosophy and approach to leading people through change, including the key factors you believe enable successful and sustainable outcomes.

3. What is one domestic macroeconomic risk that you think the Bank should be paying more attention to and why?

The RBA makes an important contribution to the Australian economy through the pursuit of national economic policy objectives and associated activities in financial markets and banking. The Opportunity

At APA, our purpose is to secure Australia's energy future. We're not doing it alone. We're doing it with our customers and communities as Australia's energy infrastructure partner, a role our unique experience and expertise positions us to play.

We now have an exciting opportunity available for a Analyst, Gas Transmission & Storage to join us in our Portfolio Strategy & Analytics team in Perth on a full-time permanent basis.

Reporting to the Manager, Gas Transmission & Storage Analytics, you will play a key role in enabling Energy Solutions' portfolio optimisation, capital prioritisation and long-term infrastructure strategy. The role delivers advanced financial modelling, scenario analysis, and strategic insights to support Final Investment Decisions, Board submissions, and Investment Committee approvals. Working across commercial, finance, and development teams, the Analyst contributes to the evaluation of large-scale gas transmission and storage infrastructure investments aligned with corporate growth objectives and regulatory frameworks.

Key Responsibilities

Develop and maintain complex financial and economic models to evaluate gas transmission and storage investment opportunities, including M&A and organic growth projects

Perform scenario modelling, market demand forecasting and sensitivity analysis to inform capital allocation and strategic portfolio prioritisation

Contribute to the preparation of Board papers, Investment Committee submissions and strategic planning documentation with robust quantitative analysis

Support post-investment performance reviews and capital deployment optimisation initiatives

Partner with commercial, finance, legal and Net Zero teams to ensure cross-functional alignment on infrastructure development strategy

Contribute to continuous improvement of analytical frameworks, valuation methodologies and market modelling tools used across the Gas portfolio

Your background

Tertiary qualification in Economics, Finance, Engineering, Mathematics, Data Analytics or related quantitative discipline

Demonstrated capability in advanced financial modelling, including discounted cash flow (DCF), IRR, NPV, and scenario analysis techniques

Strong analytical and quantitative reasoning skills with ability to interpret complex market and regulatory data

Proficiency in Excel modelling, data analytics tools and presentation software; exposure to Power BI, Python or similar analytical platforms advantageous

Understanding of infrastructure investment principles, capital markets and regulated asset environments preferred

Strong written and verbal communication skills with ability to translate technical insights into executive-level recommendations

Working at APA

At APA we're committed to fostering a safe and inclusive workplace where you can achieve your best. We'll provide you with access to targeted career development programs, competitive remuneration, employee benefits, flexible working arrangements, and wellbeing programs. We also have an inclusive parental leave policy providing 18 weeks leave including superannuation to support parents at work and in life.

We employ more than 2700 people across Australia and are committed to creating a diverse and inclusive culture, where everyone feels safe, valued, and trusted to do their best every day.

We are APA

APA Group is a leading Australian energy infrastructure business.

We proudly own, operate and manage a diverse $26 billion portfolio of gas, electricity, solar and wind assets which deliver essential services to communities and customers across Australia.

And we own more than 15,000 kilometres of gas pipelines which deliver energy to households and businesses across every corner of Australia.

As the partner of choice in delivering infrastructure solutions for the energy transition, we're focused on bundled remote grid energy solutions, electricity and gas transmission, and future energy, including hydrogen and carbon capture and storage.

Our customer centric approach means we understand our customers and offer them reliable, innovative, and cost-effective energy solutions that support their decarbonisation ambitions.

We are committed securing Australia's energy future by delivering infrastructure solutions for Australia's energy transition and supporting the decarbonisation ambitions of our customers and communities .

Join our team and be part of securing Australia's energy future.

We'd love to hear from you.

APA is an equal opportunity employer. The safety, health and wellbeing our people, community and environment a priority. We embrace diversity in our people to ensure our workforce is representative of the communities that we operate in.

Are you a data-driven digital commerce expert with a passion for creating seamless customer experiences? Do you thrive on turning insights into action, optimising every touchpoint of the customer journey to drive conversion?

 

About Omni Area

The Omni Area is IKEA’s strategic retail function responsible for delivering seamless, integrated shopping experiences across all customer touchpoints whether that is physical stores, e-commerce, pick-up points, and delivery services. This function bridges online and offline operations, ensuring customers can browse, purchase, and collect products through whichever channel suits them best. The Omni Area's core objective is to make IKEA more accessible, convenient, and affordable to customers across Australia and New Zealand, leveraging data-driven insights to optimise channel integration and enhance the end-to-end customer journey.

As the Digital CMP Optimisation Leader, you'll be at the forefront of our digital transformation, seeing the business through the eyes of the customer. You'll drive conversion and contribute to profitability by proactively analysing data, identifying opportunities, and leading improvements across all online KPIs. You're a performance-driven, solution-oriented leader who:

Defines and drives the Digital CMP performance and optimisation strategy, ensuring alignment with business priorities and customer needs while taking ownership of the online sales tree

Analyses qualitative and quantitative data to uncover customer journey pain points and inefficiencies, conducting end-to-end funnel analysis to identify drop-offs and translate insights into actionable improvements

Leads the implementation of tools, working methods, and best practices from global teams, utilising industry benchmarks to guide stakeholders and foster continuous improvement

Monitors and reports key e-commerce performance metrics with a systematic approach, providing actionable recommendations and coaching to drive accountability for results

Oversees site health monitoring, incident resolution, and platform change deployments, safeguarding checkout KPIs and ensuring operational excellence

 

About You

You are a commercially savvy digital expert with a deep understanding of online retailing and omnichannel customer journeys. You bring strong commercial acumen combined with expertise in data-driven decision-making, advanced analytics, and decision intelligence. You excel at navigating complex, dynamic business environments, driving change positively, and influencing cross-functional teams. With a proven track record of growing business and people together, you're ready to lead with IKEA values while unleashing entrepreneurial thinking across the organisation.

To be considered for this role you will have:

5+ years of experience in e-commerce, commercial leadership, or business performance management within omnichannel customer experience environments, demonstrating your depth of digital commerce expertise

Demonstrated success in optimizing online business performance and improving key performance metrics, with a proven track record of driving measurable results and conversion improvements

Strong commercial acumen and knowledge of online retailing, including current market trends, customer behaviour patterns, and competitive landscape dynamics

Strong technical proficiency in data analysis tools and programming languages, including SQL for database querying, advanced Excel for complex data manipulation and modelling, and Python (preferred) for automation and advanced analytics, along with proven experience in data visualization platforms such as Power BI, Tableau, or similar tools to create compelling insights and dashboards that drive business decisions

Expertise in omnichannel customer journeys and optimization strategies, with the ability to identify friction points and implement solutions that enhance the end-to-end shopping experience

Advanced knowledge in data-driven decision-making, analytics, and decision intelligence, including the ability to translate complex data into actionable business insights and strategic recommendations

Proven ability to actively lead, coach, and communicate effectively across cross-functional teams and global environments, providing clear points of view and inspiring action

Strong capability to navigate and lead in complex, dynamic, and continuously evolving business environments, adapting quickly to change while maintaining strategic focus

Demonstrated ability to work in agile environments, adapting strategies and tactics quickly based on feedback, changing business needs, and creating strong collaborative relationships

Knowledge of the unique IKEA range, with familiarity with IKEA values, brand positioning, and commitment to sustainability

Broad knowledge of direction setting, strategic and operational goal creation, and KPI follow-up, ensuring alignment between tactical execution and long-term business objectives


Additional information:

Permanent, full time, 76hrs/fortnight.

The location for the role is Service Office, Tempe NSW, with a minimum of 3 days a week on-site.

If you are successful for this role, you will be required to obtain a national police check.

About the role:

Experience implementing or applying data standards including data mappings, ontology, metadata, and data catalogue practices

Company description:



About us
The Department of Government Services (DGS) was established on 1 January 2023 to improve everyone's experience of doing business and interacting with the Victorian government. We bring important day-to-day services together in one department to make things easy and seamless for Victorians and businesses. We are doing this by connecting and digitising our systems and platforms across state and local government as well as streamlining our corporate and procurement services.



Job description:



About the role

As a Product Owner at DGS, you will drive the development and continuous improvement of digital products that support Victorian parents to access and understand government services. You will manage the product backlog, define the product vision and roadmap, and ensure delivery aligns with both citizen needs and whole-of-government priorities.

Working in a cross-functional environment, you will collaborate with stakeholders across government, technical teams and external partners to gather insights, prioritise enhancements and deliver high-value outcomes. You will use data, customer feedback and stakeholder input to inform decisions and improve product performance.

You will lead agile delivery practices, translate business and user needs into clear requirements, support user acceptance testing, and ensure smooth product releases and adoption.


This is a VPS5 fixed term role available for a period of 12 months.

For specific responsibilities please see the Position Description.

This position is only open to applicants with relevant rights to work in Australia.

How to apply:
Apply before the advertised closing date 11:59pm on Sunday, 12 April 2026. All applications should include:

a resume; and
a cover letter which addresses the key selection criteria (within three pages)

Other relevant information
The department is committed to providing and maintaining a working environment which is safe and without risk to the health of its employees and clients and consistent with the department's obligations under the Occupational Health and Safety Act 2004 (OH&S Act), to support this DGS has a COVID-19 Vaccination policy which strongly recommends all DGS employees be fully vaccinated.

Preferred candidates will be required to complete a Pre-employment Declaration and Consent Form. Employment of successful candidates will be subject to a National Police Check.

DGS actively promotes diversity, inclusion and an equal opportunity workplace. We welcome applicants from all diverse backgrounds, including people with disabilities and Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples. All roles at DGS can be worked flexibly, however it may differ from role to role.

About CTM 
CTM is a global award-winning provider of innovative and cost-effective travel management solutions to the corporate, events, leisure and loyalty travel markets. CTM provides personalised service excellence with client-facing technology solutions in offices all around the world, including New Zealand, Australia, Asia, North America and Europe with over 3000 employees.
  
At CTM, our people are our greatest asset. We are a team of collaborative, innovative and future focused professionals we work with the company's values in mind; Connect, Deliver and Evolve.
  
About the Role 
The Strategic Lead, leads a specialist team of Client Managers supporting CTM’s State Government contracts and travel programs, where retention is critical. It provides a single escalation point for senior government stakeholders and delivers coaching and support to Client Managers, while optimising resources and costs.

The position requires strong strategic capability to navigate the complexity of State Government travel programs and identify opportunities to increase adoption of CTM technology. It ensures alignment with operational divisions to meet and exceed service levels and contractual obligations.

The role also manages and mitigates risk through the customer risk mitigation tool and CRM and is accountable for driving revenue growth and yield in line with CTM’s five-year strategy.

Knowledge, Skills and Abilities

Highly analytical with the ability to utilise Power BI and Excel based data sources to determine priorities and actions that align with strategy.
High to Expert capability of the Microsoft suite of products, including.
o Microsoft Office
o Power BI
o Dynamics CRM
Exceptional presentation skills demonstrated via the use of Microsoft Powerpoint or similar.
Knowledge of strategic planning principles and ability to apply it when aligning the solutions for clients
High level negotiator and communicator
Exceptional organisation and time management skills proven through the daily management of critical tasks within a shared Outlook environment and tracked via one – to one meeting with your direct report as well as in Microsoft Dynamics CRM.
A bachelor’s degree in business or similar or Other qualifications such as business diploma.
Demonstrated success within the State Government sector in Client Management
Proven success in people management and leadership of State Government segment.
Why CTM?  
As a global organisation CTM offers a range of employee benefits that including;

Travel discounts  
Perkbox - Retail, Lifestyle, Entertainment, Health & Wellness discounts
Training and Development opportunities  - online lessons & certificates
Annual Volunteer Day  
x2 Wellness/Chillout Days  
Blended work arrangements with hybrid WFH flexibility 
2 Weeks extra leave - Purchase Leave
Paid Parental Leave
Sonder - EAP Platform 
CTM offer a strong, established and sustainable work environment which will support your career development and wellbeing.
  
CTM is committed to the unique contributions of all our people and actively encourage candidates with all abilities and diverse backgrounds to apply. Come work for a global award winning company that values its people, community, and technological innovation.

About Ideally

Ideally is reinventing market research for the world’s most ambitious brands. We’ve built a category-defining platform that replaces slow, siloed, and expensive legacy processes with a suite of tools that unlock opportunity for insights teams, marketers, and creative agencies.

We represent the "new school" of research. A world where brands create with their consumers, not just for them. By integrating consumer insight, category intelligence, and creativity at scale, we've empowered over 250+ clients like Google, Omnicom, Kraft Heinz, and Danone to turn raw data into bigger, bolder ideas—faster.

In just two years, we’ve expanded across 30+ countries with offices in Auckland, Sydney, Melbourne, and New York. Backed by Icehouse Ventures and OIF, we're an AI-led, fast-growing team reinventing research. This is a chance to join a company where momentum, ownership, and impact are real.

Why work at Ideally

Ideally is a place to push what’s possible. It’s for people who want to build, win, and grow while meaningfully shaping an industry and our brand. Because we hire exceptional people, we trust you with real ownership of your impact while our leaders actively provide challenge, recognition and exposure to help you keep stretching towards your potential.

This is a high-performance, high-care environment. The pace is fast and the ambition is big, but you will be surrounded by highly collaborative, smart, low-ego people who back each other, speak candidly but kindly, and celebrate wins together (for example our annual company-wide ThinkFest). With skin in the game through ESOP, global momentum, and scope to grow as we scale, Ideally is a place to build something you can be proud of while shaping your future.

While people come for our culture, team, and opportunities, we know that other perks matter too. See below for more on what we offer, and head to our Careers page for more information.

About this role

As a Senior Data Scientist (Consumer Research), you’ll play a pivotal role in turning Ideally’s growing consumer dataset into scalable product intelligence.

This is not a traditional data science role. We’re not looking for a data engineer or a product analyst. We’re looking for someone grounded in consumer research, marketing science, and applied modelling — someone who can take messy, real-world research data and turn it into meaningful, defensible insight.

You’ll work closely with Product, Research, and Engineering to design and build capabilities that help customers understand not just what happened, but why it matters — and embed that intelligence directly into the product.

This is a high-impact, highly collaborative role where you’ll partner closely with Product and Engineering leads to bring models into production and shape how insight scales across the platform.

What outcomes you’ll deliver;

Transform data into intelligence

Turn structured and unstructured research data into clear, actionable insight that customers can trust and act on.

Build scalable insight capabilities

Develop models, frameworks, and approaches that move insight from manual interpretation into embedded product features.

Strengthen and evolve existing initiatives

Improve the statistical rigour and scalability of existing and new features.

Develop new data products

Ideate and explore opportunities combining multiple, disparate data sets together into repeatable, high-value outputs.

Advance research methodologies

Modernise and extend approaches such as Conjoint, segmentation, and driver analysis to unlock deeper customer value.

Identify and build data moats

Proactively uncover opportunities where Ideally’s dataset can create a unique and compounding advantage.

Collaborate cross-functionally

Work closely with Product, Research, and Engineering to translate modelling into production-ready capabilities.


What you’ll bring

Background in quantitative consumer research, marketing science, statistics  or applied modelling

Strong understanding of survey data, experimentation, and statistical inference

Experience with techniques such as Conjoint, choice modelling, segmentation, or driver analysis

Experience working with both structured data and open-text consumer responses

Familiarity with AI and modern modelling approaches (e.g. NLP, LLMs, advanced statistical techniques)

Ability to move from analysis → insight → commercial or product impact

Creative and innovative thinker who enjoys building new approaches — not just applying standard ones

Strong collaborator who enjoys working through problems with others (not a lone operator)

Comfortable operating in ambiguity and shaping new ideas from scratch

Clear communicator who can translate complex modelling into intuitive narratives

Curious, adaptable, and motivated to create real impact

We care more about mindset, collaboration, and how you think than ticking every box of experience.

Where you’ll work

You can be based in Melbourne or Auckland, with a hybrid, office-primary culture that enables strong collaboration and flexibility. Typically, teams work remotely two days per week, with anchor office days and flexibility when needed.

We focus on impact, not hours, and trust you to manage your time and workload sustainably.

Reports to: Head of Product

What we offer as perks

Competitive rewards and upside, including salary and meaningful ESOP

Growth and progression, with stretch opportunities and exposure as we scale

Flexibility and autonomy, with a hybrid model and focus on impact rather than hours

Support that matters, including paid parental leave, fertility support schemes, and wellbeing resources

A fun, social environment, including our annual company-wide offsite ThinkFest

Perks are table stakes for us. What truly defines Ideally is our high-performance, high-care culture, the calibre of people, the ownership they’re given, and the chance to help shape an industry.

Who should apply and how to put your best foot forward

If this role or Ideally excites you and you can see yourself having an impact here, we’d love to hear from you — even if you don’t meet every single requirement listed. This job ad is a starting point for a conversation, not a rigid checklist.

We care far more about potential, mindset, and how you think than a perfect match on paper.

We also know from research that female and non-binary applicants are less likely to apply unless they feel 100% qualified. If that’s you, please take this as your nudge to apply anyway. Focus on the bullet points that reflect your strengths and experience, not the ones that don’t.

Job summary:

We are seeking an experienced SSIS Lead to drive the design, development, and optimization of our data integration and ETL processes. This role is ideal for someone who thrives on solving complex data challenges, leading technical initiatives, and ensuring the reliability and scalability of enterprise data pipelines. You’ll collaborate closely with data engineers, analysts, architects, and business stakeholders to deliver high‑quality data solutions that power critical business decisions.

Key Responsibilities:

  Lead the end‑to‑end design, development, and deployment of SSIS packages and ETL workflows.

  Establish best practices, coding standards, and performance optimization techniques for ETL processes.

  Conduct code reviews and mentor developers on SSIS, SQL, and data integration patterns.

  Build, maintain, and enhance complex SSIS packages for data extraction, transformation, and loading.

  Integrate data from multiple sources including SQL Server, APIs, flat files, cloud platforms, and third‑party systems.

  Troubleshoot and resolve ETL failures, performance bottlenecks, and data quality issues.

  Collaborate with stakeholders to design scalable and secure data integration frameworks.

  Ensure ETL processes align with data governance, security, and compliance standards.

  Maintain documentation for ETL workflows, data mappings, and operational procedures.

  Work with business teams to understand data requirements and translate them into technical solutions.

  Provide technical guidance during project planning, estimation, and solution design.

  Communicate progress, risks, and technical decisions to leadership and cross‑functional teams.

 

Skills Required:

  6+ years of hands‑on experience with SSIS, Data Modelling, Data Warehousing and SQL Server Integration Services development.

  Strong proficiency in T‑SQL, stored procedures, query optimization, and relational database concepts.

  Proven experience leading ETL or data integration projects in an enterprise environment.

  Solid understanding of data warehousing, star/snowflake schemas, and dimensional modeling.

  Experience with version control Git, CI/CD pipelines, and deployment automation.

  Familiarity with cloud data platforms (Azure Data Factory, Synapse, AWS Glue, etc.) is a strong plus.

  Excellent problem‑solving skills and the ability to work independently and collaboratively.

Next Steps:

Job Title: Senior Business Analyst – Private Health Insurance (PHI)
Ad Text:
Role Overview

We are seeking a Senior Business Analyst with strong Australian Private Health Insurance (PHI) experience, particularly across memberships, products, premiums, and claims. This role supports business and technology initiatives within a health fund environment and requires a practical understanding of how PHI operations translate into system behaviour and data outcomes.

The successful candidate will be comfortable working across core PHI processes and supporting integrations with Medicare, HICAPS, and ECLIPSE, while collaborating closely with business SMEs and delivery teams.


Key Responsibilities

Lead business analysis activities across PHI initiatives impacting membership management, product configuration, premium billing, and claims processing

Work with business stakeholders to capture and document requirements related to:

Member lifecycle events (joins, transfers, suspensions, terminations)

Product and cover structures (Hospital, Extras, combined covers)

Premium calculation, payment frequency, arrears, and advance payments

Claims submission, assessment, and benefit calculation

Analyse and document as-is and to-be processes, business rules, and system interactions

Support initiatives involving external PHI interfaces, including:

HICAPS (electronic and POS claiming)

ECLIPSE (hospital and medical claiming)

Medicare coordination and related interactions

Translate business requirements into clear functional specifications and user stories

Collaborate with solution designers, developers, and testers to ensure requirements are correctly implemented

Support SIT and UAT by defining PHI-relevant test scenarios and validating outcomes against expected business rules

Assist with implementation activities, change impacts, and post-release support


Mandatory Skills & Experience:

Proven experience as a Senior Business Analyst within the Australian PHI domain

Strong understanding of core PHI operations, including:

Membership and policy administration

Product and benefit structures

Premiums, payments, and billing cycles

Claims processing and benefit calculation

Experience supporting or analysing PHI integrations such as HICAPS, ECLIPSE, and Medicare

Ability to interpret fund rules and translate them into system and data requirements

Strong documentation skills (business requirements, functional specs, process flows, user stories)

Experience working in Agile, Waterfall, or hybrid delivery environments

Strong stakeholder engagement and communication skills


Desirable Experience

Experience working with PHI core systems.

Exposure to PHI reporting, data, or reconciliation processes

Experience supporting cutover, deployment, or go-live activities

Familiarity with common BA tooling (JIRA, Confluence, Visio, etc.)


Key Attributes

Solid understanding of PHI business processes and terminology

Analytical and detail-oriented

Comfortable working in regulated environments

Collaborative and delivery-focused

Salary: Be part of Melbourne's iconic tram team!
Ad Text:
Ding! Ding! Trams are the heartbeat of Melbourne - come be part of our team on the largest and most iconic tram network in the world.

About the Opportunity

We are seeking a highly capable Senior Data Engineer to join our Data and Integration team. This is a 2‑year fixed term role reporting to the Team Manager, Data and Integration. You will work across business and technical teams to build scalable pipelines, enhance data models, improve data quality, and support analytics tools that enable insights across Yarra Trams.

You will work across multiple Azure data services, ensuring the efficient movement, transformation and availability of data that supports operational performance, contract SLAs and broader organisational objectives. This is an opportunity to work with modern cloud technologies and contribute directly to the data capabilities that underpin Melbourne's iconic tram network.

What You Will Be Doing

Building and maintaining scalable data pipelines within Azure Data Factory.
Transforming, ingesting, and deploying large, complex datasets.
Improving internal data processes including automation and optimisation.
Supporting and maintaining data analytics tools and reporting capabilities.
Working with stakeholders to resolve data-related issues and support platform needs.
Building and maintaining data transformations, data structures, and metadata processes.
Ensuring data security classifications and controls are adhered to.
Delivering work to agreed timelines, budgets and scope.
Conducting detailed analysis and configuration of Azure data solutions.
Supporting database maintenance, security access, audit reporting and application performance monitoring.
Participating in incident and problem management as required.


What You Will Bring

Minimum 3 years' experience in a data engineering role.
Expert SQL capability and experience with relational databases.
Strong experience with Azure Data Factory and enterprise-scale data pipelines.
Experience with Azure Data Lake, data marts and Erwin modelling tools.
Proven experience building and optimising cloud data architectures in Azure.
Experience with ELT and ETL for large datasets (terabyte scale).
Strong background in data quality, data cleaning and enrichment processes.
Analytical skills and ability to work with unstructured datasets.
Strong communication skills and the ability to work with stakeholders across the business.Experience supporting BI solutions and data management strategies.

Applications close:

About the Role

As Senior Data Scientist, you will provide the deep technical expertise and theoretical rigor required to build TEG's next generation of AI products. Supporting the Principal and Lead Data Scientists, you will bridge the gap between cutting-edge research and production-ready applications. Partnering with ML Engineers and Product teams, you will ensure technical excellence through the implementation of production-grade CI/CD, Dockerisation, and robust model monitoring. Your primary goal is to elevate the team’s technical execution, ensuring every solution is built on a foundation of scientific excellence and scalable engineering.



Requirements



What a day in the life looks like?

SOTA Implementation: Implement state-of-the-art (SOTA) machine learning and Generative AI solutions and apply deep theoretical understanding to develop pragmatic solutions spanning from traditional machine learning to advanced GenAI techniques that drive real business outcomes.
Solution Design Support: Partner with the Principal and Lead Data Scientists to provide technical feasibility assessments and research-backed insights for the AI roadmap.
Experimentation & Measurement: Implement robust frameworks experimentation and measurement frameworks to quantitatively measure model performance and overall business impact.
Development and Operational Delivery: Write production-quality code to develop and refine models, partnering with ML Engineers to deploy these solutions in cloud platforms.
Vertex AI Mastery: Design and implement end-to-end AI solutions using the full Vertex AI ecosystem, including Pipelines, Agent Garden, and Vertex Search.
Stakeholder Collaboration: Act as a trusted advisor, translating complex model behaviours and AI-driven business impacts into actionable insights that align technical delivery with TEG's broader commercial strategy.

About you

Essential experience & skills:

Minimum 5 years experience in delivering high-impact data science projects from inception to production, ideally within a high-volume e-commerce or digital environment.
A tertiary degree in a quantitative field such as Statistics, Mathematics, Engineering, Computer Science, or a related discipline.
Significant theoretical and practical experience in at least two of our core delivery areas: recommendation engines, pricing optimisation, or customer lifecycle modeling. Proven experience building GenAI and Agentic AI solutions is a plus.
Proven ability to design experimental frameworks and apply statistical methods to validate model performance and business uplift.
Advanced skills in BigQuery and Python, and hands-on experience with GCP and Vertex AI.
Extensive experience working in a collaborative software development environment, including version control (Git) and CI/CD pipelines.
Possess the ability to communicate technical concepts and experimental outcomes to stakeholders at all levels.

About the Role

Our client is seeking a highly analytical and systems-oriented Tenure Specialist to oversee the delivery, enhancement, and integrity of global tenure management systems. This role is pivotal in ensuring that exploration activities remain compliant across multiple international jurisdictions while driving technical innovation in how land and contract data are managed.

The successful candidate will act as the bridge between technical GIS teams, legal/commercial departments, and field-based exploration units. This is not merely an administrative position; it is a systems-leadership role designed to improve productivity and ensure the security of the company's global resource footprint.

Primary Responsibilities

The Tenure Specialist is responsible for the end-to-end health of the tenure and contract management ecosystem. Key duties include:

System Administration & Integrity: Managing system use and data quality through KPIs and metrics, moving the organization toward a self-service data model.

Technical Support & Innovation: Providing hands-on troubleshooting and developing improvements for tenure solutions, specifically focusing on integration with GIS and Business Intelligence (BI) tools.

Governance & Compliance: Implementing and tracking technical standards and workflows based on specific jurisdictional requirements.

Accountability & Reporting: Developing frameworks to measure data completeness. The Specialist will proactively hold users accountable for action alerts and ensure all legal content is secured.

Analytics for Decision Making: Delivering high-level analytics (e.g., tenure-over-time, "coming free" reports, and TARPs) to support executive decision-making.

Training & Development: Equipping end-users with the skills and knowledge required to maintain data standards globally.

Capabilities and Experience

To be successful in this role, the candidate must demonstrate a blend of technical rigour and stakeholder influence:

Technical Expertise: In-depth experience with Geographic Information Systems (GIS) and the application of data management principles to tenure and contract data.

Jurisdictional Knowledge: Proven ability to manage and update risk information systems in response to legislative changes across various global regions.

Data Governance: Experience setting and monitoring clear data governance frameworks integrated with broader project management systems (IPMF).

Analytical Skills: Proficiency in temporal and spatial data analysis to provide insights into spend commitments and land obligations.

Stakeholder Engagement: Ability to collaborate effectively with IS&T, Land, Commercial, and Digital teams on a global scale.


If you have the technical GIS background and the governance expertise required to safeguard global exploration assets, please submit your CV

The opportunity at OneStop:

We're looking for a commercially sharp Group Product Manager to lead the Data & Innovation portfolio — a collection of products that turn OneStop’s unique data assets into intelligence, automation, and new revenue streams.

 

This is not a technical custodian role. You'll be as fluent talking to the Board about a new revenue line as you are in a customer feedback session. You'll own the full product lifecycle: strategy, build, pricing, packaging, and go to market — with a tribe of product managers, engineers, and QA practitioners looking to you for direction and commercial rigour.

 

What you’ll own:

Portfolio strategy: Translate the corporate strategy into a 12-24 month horizon strategic roadmap for the Tribe , aligned to Company & Board level objectives & KPIs, incorporating AI/ML opportunities to drive the growth of the portfolio.
GTM: Lead GTM planning for new data and analytics products — including launch sequencing, customer segmentation, messaging, and sales enablement.
Communication (path to vision): Champion the future state of the product portfolio to internal and external stakeholders, highlighting the role of AI/ML in achieving product goals, and clearly articulating the commercial ‘Why?’ behind the roadmap.
Market Intelligence: Drive research into market trends to identify unmet needs / opportunities and inform product strategy and investment cases, ensuring OneStop maintains defensive moat through data and AI and informs growth strategy.
Pricing & packaging architecture: Design and optimise product packaging and tiering (eg, Good/Better/Best & how that translates into tiering for new product offerings) that maximises Lifetime Value (LTV) and captures the unique ROI of our AI insights.
Monetisation Engine: Lead the transition or introduction of new business models (eg, shifting from transactional to subscription/value based models).
GTM & Commercial Readiness: Own the end to end GTM execution. This goes beyond ‘technical’ planning. Lead GTM planning for new data and analytics products — including launch sequencing, customer segmentation, messaging, and sales enablement.
Yield Optimisation: Establish rigorous post launch feedback loops to iterate on pricing elasticity, friction points, and product market fit.
Value-Driven AI: Identify and evaluate AI/ML opportunities, defining clear value propositions and success metrics that move beyond "cool tech" to "commercial outcome."
Data Services Advocacy: Advocate for a data driven approach, leading the execution of data powered experiences that support the deployment of predictive logistics solutions.
Performance Monitoring: Analyse the commercial performance of AI/ML solutions, iterating to maximise both customer effectiveness and business value.
P&L Accountability: Manage Tribe P&Ls, tracking margins, reducing costs, and identifying new revenue generating opportunities.
Board Level Business Cases: Develop robust investment cases and ROI evaluations for new opportunities, providing clear sensitivity analysis on pricing and adoption assumptions.
Joint Accountability: Share responsibility with Commercial and Growth teams for delivering specific EBITDA and market share outcomes.
Talent Mentorship: Develop and mentor Product Managers, fostering a culture of high performance and customer centricity.
Cultural Transformation: Play a key role in transitioning OneStop into a truly product led organisation by shaping new processes and cross functional ways of working

 

What we’re looking for?

Depth of experience with 5+ years leading major product initiatives across multiple B2B products. You can take rough times and big ideas and turn them into a product workplan, roadmap linked to strategic pillars, an experiment map, and research with minimal guidance.
Business Impact focus and a consistent history of delivering measurable results for both users, customers and the business.
GTM leadership: You have designed and executed go-to-market plans for B2B products — not delegated them. You know what a launch plan looks like and you've owned the result.
Pricing & packaging: You've designed commercial models from scratch — tiered pricing, usage-based, bundled — with documented rationale and commercial outcomes. Not just a slide; lived experience.
Stakeholder influence: You've navigated complex, multi-stakeholder environments including C-suite and Board-level audiences. You adapt your communication style without losing your point of view.
Data & platform products: You understand data products — APIs, analytics, intelligence layers — and can hold your own with data engineering and architecture teams without needing to be one of them.
Strong prioritisation skills ensuring that your product workplan is focused on high impact, urgent and commercially feasible product work.
Data minded wherever possible. You deeply understand how your products work and how to measure long term impact. You understand the value of data capture and value creation.
Ability to think big and small to build a portfolio of features and experiments. You are comfortable whiteboarding a completely new experience as you are optimizing an existing flow.
Product interaction and craftsmanship. You will own every product interaction across your remit and should show the attention to detail this responsibility deserves.
Natural leadership. You’ll know how to influence people, collaborate, get buy-in as required, to get things done. People will enjoy working with you. Stakeholder management is a core part of your forte.
Regular written and verbal communication, that is clear and effective, will be required to ensure you are aligned with your internal and external stakeholders and customers.
Logistics, supply chain or regulated industry: Experience in freight, ports, trade finance, or comparable B2B environments. You understand multi-sided platforms and enterprise buying cycles.

 

 

 

What’s next?

The Opportunity 

QIC is seeking a senior leader to take ownership of our newly created position -  Head of Enterprise Initiative & Governance. This is a high-impact role at the centre of our organisation, responsible for ensuring QIC is focused on the right priorities and delivering on its strategy. 

This is a rare opportunity to build and shape a new enterprise function from the ground up within a leading global investment organisation. 

Reporting to the Chief Financial and Operating Officer (CFOO), this role will bring greater clarity, structure and discipline to how work is prioritised, governed and delivered across the business. It will provide a unique opportunity to shape how QIC executes its strategy, with direct visibility to senior leadership and enterprise-wide influence. 

About you 

You will play a critical role in connecting strategy to execution, bringing together enterprise prioritisation, portfolio oversight and performance insight to enable better decision-making and stronger outcomes. In this role, you will also help align investment, client, corporate and government priorities, ensuring a clear and consistent strategic narrative for key stakeholders. 

Using your previous experience of establishing an enterprise-wide PMO, you will create capability, and visibility across all initiatives, ensuring they are prioritised, sequenced and resourced effectively. Working closely with senior leaders across QIC, you will drive a more coordinated and disciplined approach to delivery, improving how the organisation makes decisions and allocates resources. 

The role offers significant scope to influence how QIC operates at an enterprise level, balancing strategic thinking with hands-on execution, and working across a complex, high-performing environment. 

Key Responsibilities 

Translate QIC’s strategy into clear, actionable enterprise priorities, ensuring alignment across investment, corporate and government initiatives 

Develop and embed an enterprise-wide prioritisation framework, balancing strategic alignment, value, risk, resourcing and sequencing of work 

Establish and lead an enterprise PMO capability, creating visibility across all initiatives and improving governance, consistency and delivery outcomes 

Provide a consolidated, enterprise-wide view of strategic initiatives, enabling informed decision-making at ExCo and Board level 

Implement structured governance including business case standards, stage gates, approval pathways and measures of success 

Drive benefits realisation, post-implementation reviews and continuous improvement across initiatives 

Partner with Finance and People & Culture to align strategy execution with budgets, workforce planning and capacity 

Define and track enterprise KPIs and performance metrics, linking strategy execution to outcomes and accountability 

Provide independent challenge and insight to ensure initiatives deliver value and align with strategic priorities 

Act as a central connector across the organisation, working closely with senior stakeholders to drive alignment, prioritisation and execution discipline 

Key Criteria

Tertiary qualifications in Business, Finance, or related discipline 

10+ years’ experience in strategy execution, portfolio management or business performance  

Strong commercial and project management skills 

Highly developed analytical and problem-solving skills including a solution orientated mindset  

Demonstrated experience operating in complex, multi-stakeholder environments  

Excellent prioritisation and organisational skills 

Strong written and interpersonal skills with demonstrated ability to influence and negotiate at senior leadership levels

Company Description

Nine is Australia’s largest locally owned media company. Working at Nine, you’ll have access to a unique range of experiences and opportunities, helping drive the success of the country’s most trusted television, radio, digital and publishing brands. 

Our content reaches almost every Australian - meaning what we do has real impact. We bring people together, celebrate the big moments, and capture the everyday ones.

Some of our most beloved brands have been part of Australian life for generations, and others - new on the scene - have already found their place firmly in our lives. We’re evolving and we need people like you to bring new ideas, innovate and make your mark.

Job Description

At Nine, we are harnessing the power of Artificial Intelligence to revolutionise our audience engagement and commercial strategies. As Head of AI Product, you will be at the forefront of this transformation, driving the development and implementation of innovative AI-powered products across our diverse portfolio.

This role is pivotal in shaping Nine’s AI strategy, translating complex AI capabilities into tangible product features that deliver exceptional value to our audiences, advertisers, and internal teams. You will be responsible for defining the product vision, building the roadmap, and leading a cross-functional team to deliver cutting-edge AI solutions.

Reporting to the Director, AI and Data Product, you will work closely with data scientists, engineers, and business stakeholders to identify and capitalise on opportunities to leverage AI in our media landscape.

As Head of AI Product, you will:

Define and champion the AI product vision and strategy: Align AI initiatives with Nine’s overall business objectives, identifying key opportunities to leverage AI for innovation and competitive advantage.

Lead the development of AI-powered products: Drive the end-to-end product lifecycle, from ideation and prototyping to launch and iteration, ensuring products are aligned with user needs and deliver measurable value.

Collaborate with cross-functional teams: Work closely with data scientists, engineers, designers, and business stakeholders to define requirements, prioritise features, and deliver high-quality AI solutions.

Build and maintain the AI product roadmap: Prioritise initiatives based on business impact, technical feasibility, and customer feedback, ensuring a clear and achievable roadmap.

Drive AI adoption across the organization: Evangelise the potential of AI, providing guidance and resources to teams across Nine to explore and integrate AI capabilities into their workflows.

Implement AI governance and ethical practices: Ensure AI products are developed and deployed responsibly, adhering to ethical guidelines and data privacy regulations

Analyse and measure product performance: Define and track key metrics to evaluate the success of AI products, using data-driven insights to inform product decisions and drive continuous improvement.

Stay ahead of the curve: Continuously research and evaluate emerging AI technologies and trends, identifying opportunities to incorporate them into Nine’s product strategy.

Qualifications

We are seeking a passionate and driven product leader with a strong understanding of AI and its potential to transform the media industry. You will have a proven track record of delivering successful AI products and a deep understanding of the product development lifecycle.

What you’ll bring to the role:

Experience:

5+ years of experience in product management, with a focus on AI or machine learning products.
Experience working in a media, technology, or data-driven organisation.
Experience leading cross-functional teams and managing complex projects.
Demonstrated experience bringing AI products from conception to launch.

Skills:

Deep understanding of AI and machine learning concepts and technologies.
Strong product vision and strategic thinking, with the ability to translate business objectives into actionable product roadmaps.
Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to influence and collaborate with diverse teams.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with a data-driven approach to decision-making.
Understanding of data governance, privacy, and ethical considerations related to AI.Familiarity with agile product development methodologies.

Qualifications:

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Engineering, or a related field.
Masters or PhD in AI/ML is highly desirable.
Certifications in AI product management or machine learning are advantageous.

About Ausgrid – we’re wired for good

We’re the largest distributer of electricity on Australia’s east coast, which means over two million homes and businesses rely on our good work. We’re here at the heart of the energy transition, and we’re driven by a powerful purpose to make electricity accessible for all.

It’s an exciting new chapter for our company with each day bringing a renewed energy to how we deliver, store and use electricity. As we continue to grow, we’re in search of exceptional talent to help us achieve our goals.

Everyone’s job is important here. Our customers come from all walks of life, and so do we. We’re committed to fostering a diverse, respectful, and inclusive workplace, and encourage applications from all backgrounds.

 

See yourself working here

We’re seeking a Regulatory Reset Analyst to play a key role in shaping Ausgrid’s forthcoming regulatory proposal. This position supports the development of high quality financial and regulatory analysis and modelling that meets Australian Energy Regulator (AER) requirements while delivering strong commercial outcomes for the business.

Working closely with internal stakeholders across Finance, Treasury and Network & Digital teams, senior management, as well as external parties including regulators and consultants, you’ll contribute to regulatory submissions that have a direct impact on Ausgrid’s forecast revenue and long-term business strategy.

Your key responsibilities will be:

Prepare financial and regulatory analysis for Ausgrid’s standard control services (SCS) and alternative control services (ACS) regulatory proposals

Support AER submissions through drafting, stakeholder engagement and consultant coordination

Modelling of SCS and ACS regulatory revenues and prices

Validate and quality assure inputs to regulatory models

Provide regulatory inputs into corporate financial models

Prepare revenue data for the Reset Regulatory Information Notice (Reset RIN)

Assisting with the development of a databook that will collect the data to be used for Ausgrid’s Regulatory Proposal

Collaborate with the Pricing team to support customer impact and forecast revenue analysis

Analyse regulatory information to strengthen Ausgrid’s regulatory position.




What you bring to the role

You are an analytical and commercially minded professional with a strong interest in regulation, and financial modelling. You enjoy working with complex data and financial models, can translate technical concepts into clear insights, and are comfortable engaging with a wide range of stakeholders.

To be successful, you will have:

Bachelor’s degree in Economics, Finance, Commerce, Accounting or a related discipline

Advanced proficiency in Excel and financial modelling, ideally including revenue forecasting

Strong analytical skills with a high attention to detail and ability to reconcile data and assumptions

Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to explain complex concepts in plain English

Strong interpersonal skills and the ability to build effective working relationships across teams

Proven ability to manage priorities, meet deadlines and work in a dynamic environment

Preferably have knowledge/understanding in Python

Preferably have knowledge/understanding of the energy regulatory arrangements.

At eCom Capital, growth is oxygen, and performance marketing is the engine that feeds the business. Every lead, every booked call, every dollar spent, every percentage shift in CAC and ROAS directly impacts a real person betting on themselves to change their life.

The Senior Growth Marketer is responsible for making the marketing machine run — fast, efficient, and profitable.

This role exists to turn paid spend into qualified call volume… and qualified call volume into new clients for our sales department.

No fluff. No waiting. No excuses. Only growth.




Why This Role Matters

If you are obsessed with numbers, iteration, CAC, CPL, ROAS, attribution, conversion rates, creative performance, and optimisation, this is the kind of role where you get to create enormous commercial impact every week.

You will:

Manage large-scale budgets across Meta and Google

Launch, scale, and optimize campaigns on a weekly cycle.

Drive daily acquisition volume for the sales team.

Liaise with other teams & departments (product, sales, CS) for the most accurate feedback, up to date timing of changes, iterations, product updates, communication styles and client results that can be shared within marketing dept arsenal.

Make real-time decisions that directly increase company revenue, cash collection, and profitability.

Build systems that turn experiments into frameworks and frameworks into predictable growth.

Take ownership of marketing team members (email, content, tech) within the marketing dept to drive the marketing system as a whole (not just manage ad accounts)

Report on performance, strategise weekly optimisations, run event promotions, your marketing skillset will be tested and stretched.

You won't just run ads.
You will fuel the engine that builds the company.




Key Responsibilities
1. Lead the Paid Media Engine

Own performance across the full acquisition funnel:

Meta, Google & lead expansion into other platforms

Daily optimisation across targeting, bids, budgets, and audiences

Maintain minimum daily spend efficiency required to sustain acquisition targets

Pipeline ownership: leads → MQL → SQL → show-ups → CAC → ROAS


2. Manage & Scale Campaigns

Deploy, test, and iterate new creative variations weekly

Scale winning angles fast and cut losses faster

Maintain efficient CAC while driving volume for sales

Build "launch → analyse → optimise → scale" frameworks


3. Creative Strategy & CRO

Work with creative team to produce new hooks, angles, iterations

Implement funnel CRO, success-page tests, landing page experiments

Own data-driven decision making around creative formats and messaging


4. Reporting, Analytics & Attribution

Produce weekly reporting pack (executive-level)

Live attribution monitoring via Hyros & GA4

Build dashboards for daily clarity and speed

Translate metrics into actions (not just data dumps)


Weekly reporting includes:

Ad spend

Leads

CPL

Booked calls

CPA & CAC

ROAS

Revenue impact

Weekly experiments

Winning creatives

Blockers, insights, next week's optimisations


5. Performance Execution & Continuous Improvement

Identify CPM/CPL/CAC trends and act immediately

Re-engage lost leads (no-show & cancel sequences)

Improve show-up rates via funnel & comms optimisation

Maintain consistent SQL quality

Execute during peak cost periods (e.g., Black Friday) with stability


Expected Weekly & Monthly Outputs

This is a performance role. You'll be measured on the following:

Acquisition Metrics

CPL efficiency vs. target

CPA efficiency vs. target

CAC efficiency vs. target

Show-up rate optimisation

Booked calls volume weekly (target VS actual)

Spend & Scale

Maintain and scale 5 figure daily budgets with profitable ROAS

Experience managing daily advertising budgets in the $10,000-$30,000 range is essential for this role

Keep CAC within target ranges

Increase quality & quantity of SQLs each week

Creative Execution

Minimum weekly creative iterations tested

Identify & scale winning hooks, angles, and formats

Maintain backlog of test-ready scripts, angles, and variations with creative ideation/scripting team to drive better results using data analysis of what worked & why.

Strategic Contributions

Funnel improvements (entire funnel mapping & optimisation)

Mentoring junior media buyers & creative team to drive better performance

Supporting major campaign launches, webinars, product launches

Work closely with the CEO to sync across departmental opportunities for marketing involvement opportunities.

Aligning tightly with sales to ensure downstream success


Weekly Experiments & Levers You Will Pull

The Head of Growth Marketing will own the initiative lists such as:

Improving sales comms → higher show rates

Re-engagement sequences for no-show & cancel leads (20% recovery expected)

Success page video tests (testing VSL scripts to improve throughput)

Funnel A/Bs

Audience & bidding strategy tests

Creative angle stress-testing (iteration with creative team to drive better results)
Offer-specific landing page variations


Your job:

Run tests → extract insights → roll out the winners → kill the losers → repeat weekly.




Who You Are

You might be a fit if:

You've run large budgets at scale for lead generation/consulting businesses

A minimum of 5+ years' experience managing large-scale performance marketing budgets is required

You're fast, analytical, commercially astute

You think in numbers, systems, and frameworks

You move aggressively and solve problems before they appear

You improve daily: skills, strategy, performance, decision-making

You treat marketing like a sport/game, with weekly scoreboards & high degrees of ownership on everything that you do (never blaming or dismissing an opportunity to take ownership)

You love competitive environments and high standards & love being pushed to become great.

You're fluent in attribution, analytics, tracking, and CRO

You want a pathway from Head of Growth Marketing → Director of Growth → Director of Growth → CMO

A full-time position exists for a Business Analyst to join the Technology stream.



Job description:



As a Business Analyst, Technology, you will act as a critical liaison between business stakeholders and technical teams, responsible for gathering, analysing, and translating complex business needs into actionable functional and non-functional requirements within an agile environment. You will facilitate workshops to elicit requirements, support project scope definition, and oversee user acceptance testing (UAT) to ensure solutions align with business objectives. The ideal candidate will possess strong analytical & process mapping skills, proficiency in requirements management tools like JIRA and Confluence, and the ability to manage multiple priorities under pressure. While experience in system integration projects and knowledge of energy market operations is highly regarded, we are specifically looking for a collaborative professional with a proven ability to foster true partnerships and drive process improvements across the technology delivery lifecycle.

Key Responsibilities:

Collaborating with business stakeholders to elicit, analyse, and validate detailed functional and non-functional requirements through workshops, meetings, and interviews.
Develop and maintain current and future state process models to support solution design and business understanding.
Use process modelling to drive clarity, alignment, and decision-making, not just documentation.
Assisting with the definition of project scope and objectives, while also supporting test cases and user acceptance testing (UAT).
Identifying opportunities to enhance the technology delivery lifecycle and maintaining high standards for documentation and analytical rigor within the team.
Proactively identifying complex technical and business issues, assessing risks related to project scope, and proposing effective mitigation strategies.
Acting as a critical link between business users and technology teams, ensuring requirements are effectively communicated to developers and quality assurance.
Staying current with industry best practices in agile methodologies and ensuring all work aligns with company values and safety commitments.
Contribute to continuous improvement of BA practices, templates, and ways of working.

About the location:

The location for this role is Melbourne, supporting a strong, collaborative team environment allowing you to build robust relationships with stakeholders and acting as a critical link between business users and technical teams.

The roster is Monday to Friday, with a minimum requirement of 3 days per week in the office (Hybrid Role).

About you:

You have a solid foundation in Business Analysis, with 3-5 years experience working on technology or digital delivery initiatives, with ability to mentor team members.
You have strong process modelling skills, with experience mapping end-to-end business processes & also use process modelling as a tool for analysis and improvement, not just documentation.
You can identify gaps, inefficiencies, and opportunities through process analysis.
You are comfortable working across both business and technical domains, with an interest in how systems and processes operate.
You have exposure to system integrations, data flows, or multi-system environments.
You can translate complex concepts into clear, structured requirements.
You have experience using tools such as JIRA and Confluence.
You are a strong collaborator who works effectively as part of a team and contributes positively to team outcomes.
You build strong relationships with stakeholders and communicate clearly and confidently.
You are comfortable asking questions and challenging assumptions in a constructive way.
You take ownership of your work, follow through on commitments, and can manage multiple priorities.
You are adaptable and able to navigate ambiguity in a changing environment.
You demonstrate a growth mindset and are open to feedback and continuous improvement.
Experience working on complex or multi-system projects (desirable).
Experience in energy, utilities, or infrastructure sectors (desirable).
Exposure to Agile or hybrid delivery environments (desirable).
Experience working with vendors or third-party solution providers (desirable).
Formal Business Analysis qualifications or certifications (e.g., IIBA) (desirable).
